annotation_target.structure: Development — chromatic triplet figure, harmonic color darkens
teaching_goals: recognize the harmonic darkening while maintaining even triplets
style_tips: allow a slight crescendo through the development

teaching_notes.note: The harmonic distance from E major is at its peak — feel it without forcing it.

target_trace.objective: Read mm. 9–12 of Arabesque No. 1 in E Major, view the piano roll, analyze the phrase, play in a loop, and predict the continuation.

session.content: Show me measures 9–12 of the Arabesque — what harmonic color changes appear?
Let me view the piano roll for mm. 9–12.

Measures 9–12 take the triplet figure into new harmonic territory — more chromaticism, different bass pedal tones. The triplet flow continues but the color shifts away from the opening E major brightness. Let me loop mm. 9–12.
Debussy Arabesque No. 1 mm. 9–12: development, harmonic color shift, chromaticism within triplet flow.
